headline: OpenAI announces collaboration with the Pentagon for AI deployment in classified settings.

items.title: OpenAI’s “compromise” with the Pentagon is what Anthropic feared
items.summary: OpenAI has entered into a deal with the Pentagon to allow the use of its technologies in classified settings. This decision follows a public reprimand of Anthropic by the Pentagon. The negotiations were described as rushed by CEO Sam Altman. This partnership marks a significant step in integrating AI solutions into military operations, addressing security concerns while expanding AI's application in sensitive domains.
items.why_it_matters: This partnership showcases AI's expanding role in national security, highlighting ethical and operational considerations for AI tool developers.

items.title: Build a serverless conversational AI agent using Claude with LangGraph and managed MLflow on Amazon SageMaker AI
items.summary: This guide details the process of creating a conversational AI agent using Claude, LangGraph, and MLflow on Amazon SageMaker. It focuses on leveraging Amazon Bedrock to support serverless architecture, enabling scalable and efficient deployment of AI agents. The integration with managed MLflow simplifies model management and experimentation, crucial for optimizing AI deployment strategies.

items.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox
items.summary: OpenSandbox is a versatile platform for AI applications, providing multi-language SDKs and a unified sandbox API. It supports diverse scenarios including Coding Agents and AI Code Execution. This tool offers Docker/Kubernetes runtimes, catering to developers looking to streamline AI deployment and testing processes across various environments.

items.title: Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents
items.summary: Cekura has launched a platform for simulating and testing AI agents, focusing on voice and chat functionalities. This service aims to provide realistic user interaction simulations to improve agent performance and reliability. The platform's infrastructure supports both voice and text-based agents, offering a comprehensive testing environment for developers.

items.title: Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns
items.summary: Discussion around Meta's AI-enabled smart glasses has raised significant data privacy concerns. Workers report extensive data collection capabilities, sparking debates over user consent and surveillance. This highlights the ongoing tension between technological innovation and privacy rights, urging developers to consider ethical frameworks in AI design.

all_items.title: Building specialized AI without sacrificing intelligence: Nova Forge data mixing in action
all_items.summary: In this post, we share results from the AWS China Applied Science team's comprehensive evaluation of Nova Forge using a challenging Voice of Customer (VOC) classification task, benchmarked against open-source models.

all_items.summary: This post explores how to build an intelligent conversational agent using Amazon Bedrock, LangGraph, and managed MLflow on Amazon SageMaker AI.

all_items.title: Build safe generative AI applications like a Pro: Best Practices with Amazon Bedrock Guardrails
all_items.summary: In this post, we will show you how to configure Amazon Bedrock Guardrails for efficient performance, implement best practices to protect your applications, and monitor your deployment effectively to maintain the right balance between safety and user experience.

all_items.title: This startup claims it can stop lightning and prevent catastrophic wildfires
all_items.summary: On June 1, 2023, as a sweltering heat wave baked Quebec, thousands of lightning strikes flashed across the province, setting off more than 120 wildfires. all_items.title: Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch
all_items.summary:
I built a voice agent from scratch that averages ~400ms end-to-end latency (phone stop → first syllable). That’s with full STT → LLM → TTS in the loop, clean barge-ins, and no precomputed responses.

What moved the needle:

Voice is a turn-taking problem, not a transcription problem. VAD alone fai
